controls.description: Employ organization-defined out-of-band channels for the physical delivery or electronic transmission of organization-defined information, system components, or devices to organization-defined individuals or systems.

controls.title: Operations Security
controls.description: Employ organization-defined operations security controls to protect key organizational information throughout the system development life cycle.

controls.title: Process Isolation
controls.description: Maintain a separate execution domain for each executing system process.

controls.title: Wireless Link Protection
controls.description: Protect external and internal wireless links from organization-defined types of signal parameter attacks or references to sources for such attacks.

controls.title: Port and I/O Device Access
controls.description: Physically or logically disable or remove organization-defined connection ports or input/output devices on organization-defined systems or system components.

controls.title: Sensor Capability and Data
controls.description: Prohibit the use of devices possessing organization-defined environmental sensing capabilities in organization-defined facilities, areas, or systems; and prohibit the remote activation of environmental sensing capabilities on organizational systems or system components.

controls.title: Usage Restrictions
controls.description: Establish usage restrictions and implementation guidelines for organization-defined system components; and authorize, monitor, and control the use of such components within the system.

controls.title: Detonation Chambers
controls.description: Employ a detonation chamber capability within organization-defined systems, system components, or locations.

controls.title: System Time Synchronization
controls.description: Synchronize system clocks within and between systems and system components.

controls.title: Cross Domain Policy Enforcement
controls.description: Implement a policy enforcement mechanism between the connected domains or systems with organization-defined rules.

controls.title: Alternate Communications Paths
controls.description: Establish organization-defined alternate communications paths for system operations organizational command and control.

controls.title: Sensor Relocation
controls.description: Relocate organization-defined sensors and monitoring capabilities to organization-defined locations under organization-defined conditions or circumstances.

controls.title: Hardware-Enforced Separation and Policy Enforcement
controls.description: Implement hardware-based mechanisms that enforce separation and policy enforcement.

controls.title: Software-Enforced Separation and Policy Enforcement
controls.description: Implement software-based mechanisms that enforce separation and policy enforcement.

controls.title: Hardware-Based Protection
controls.description: Employ hardware-based protection mechanisms to protect against identified threats.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate a system and information integrity policy and procedures that address purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance.

controls.title: Flaw Remediation
controls.description: Identify, report, and correct system flaws; test software and firmware updates related to flaw remediation for effectiveness and potential side effects before installation; install security-relevant software and firmware updates within an organization-defined time period of the release of the updates; and incorporate flaw remediation into the organizational configuration management process.

controls.title: Malicious Code Protection
controls.description: Implement organization-defined signature-based or non-signature-based malicious code protection mechanisms at system entry and exit points to detect and eradicate malicious code; automatically update malicious code protection mechanisms as new releases are available in accordance with organizational configuration management policy and procedures; configure malicious code protection mechanisms to perform periodic scans of the system and real-time scans of files from external sources at endpoints or network entry and exit points; and address the receipt of false positives during malicious code detection and eradication and the resulting potential impact on the availability of the system.

controls.title: System Monitoring
controls.description: Monitor the system to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks in accordance with organization-defined monitoring objectives; and identify unauthorized use of the system.

controls.title: Security Alerts, Advisories, and Directives
controls.description: Receive system security alerts, advisories, and directives from organization-defined external organizations on an ongoing basis; generate internal security alerts, advisories, and directives as deemed necessary; disseminate security alerts, advisories, and directives to organization-defined personnel, roles, and external organizations; and implement security directives in accordance with established time frames, or notify the issuing organization of the degree of noncompliance.

controls.title: Security and Privacy Function Verification
controls.description: Verify the correct operation of organization-defined security and privacy functions; perform the verification at an organization-defined frequency; alert organization-defined personnel or roles to failed security and privacy verification tests; and shut the system down, restart the system, or perform organization-defined alternative actions when anomalies are discovered.

controls.title: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity
controls.description: Employ integrity verification tools to detect unauthorized changes to organization-defined software, firmware, and information; and take organization-defined actions when unauthorized changes to the software, firmware, and information are detected.

controls.title: Spam Protection
controls.description: Employ spam protection mechanisms at system entry and exit points to detect and act on unsolicited messages; and update spam protection mechanisms when new releases are available in accordance with organizational configuration management policy and procedures.

controls.title: Information Input Validation
controls.description: Check the validity of organization-defined information inputs.

controls.title: Error Handling
controls.description: Generate error messages that provide information necessary for corrective actions without revealing information that could be exploited; and reveal error messages only to organization-defined personnel or roles.

controls.title: Information Management and Retention
controls.description: Manage and retain information within the system and information output from the system in accordance with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, guidelines, and operational requirements.

controls.title: Predictable Failure Prevention
controls.description: Determine mean time to failure for organization-defined system components in specific environments of operation; and provide substitute system components and a means to exchange active and standby components in accordance with organization-defined mean time to failure substitution criteria.

controls.title: Non-Persistence
controls.description: Implement organization-defined non-persistent system components and services that are initiated in a known state and terminated upon end of session of use or at an organization-defined frequency.

controls.title: Information Output Filtering
controls.description: Validate information output from organization-defined software programs and applications to ensure that the information is consistent with the expected content.

controls.title: Memory Protection
controls.description: Implement organization-defined controls to protect the system memory from unauthorized code execution.

controls.title: Fail-Safe Procedures
controls.description: Implement the organization-defined fail-safe procedures when organization-defined failure conditions occur.

controls.title: Personally Identifiable Information Quality Operations
controls.description: Check the accuracy, relevance, timeliness, and completeness of personally identifiable information across the information life cycle at an organization-defined frequency; and correct or delete inaccurate or outdated personally identifiable information.

controls.title: De-Identification
controls.description: Remove personally identifiable information from datasets at an organization-defined frequency and using organization-defined techniques for de-identification.

controls.title: Tainting
controls.description: Embed organization-defined tainting tools in the following systems or system components to identify the following datasets.

controls.title: Information Refresh
controls.description: Refresh organization-defined information at organization-defined frequencies or generate the information on demand and delete the information when no longer needed.

controls.title: Information Diversity
controls.description: Employ organization-defined alternative sources for organization-defined essential functions and corroborate the obtained information.

controls.title: Information Fragmentation
controls.description: Based on organization-defined circumstances, fragment organization-defined information as follows: fragment the information into organization-defined elements; and distribute the fragmented information across organization-defined systems or system components.

controls.title: Policy and Procedures
controls.description: Develop, document, and disseminate a supply chain risk management policy and procedures that address purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance.

controls.title: Supply Chain Risk Management Plan
controls.description: Develop a plan for managing supply chain risks associated with the research and development, design, manufacturing, acquisition, delivery, integration, operations and maintenance, and disposal of organization-defined systems, system components, or system services.

controls.title: Supply Chain Controls and Processes
controls.description: Establish and apply a process for identifying and addressing weaknesses or deficiencies in the supply chain elements and processes; employ organization-defined supply chain controls to protect against supply chain risks; and document the selected and implemented supply chain processes and controls.

controls.title: Acquisition Strategies, Tools, and Methods
controls.description: Employ organization-defined acquisition strategies, contract tools, and procurement methods to protect against, identify, and mitigate supply chain risks.

controls.title: Supplier Assessments and Reviews
controls.description: Assess and review the supply chain-related risks associated with suppliers or contractors and the system, system component, or system service they provide at an organization-defined frequency.

controls.title: Supply Chain Operations Security
controls.description: Employ organization-defined operations security controls to protect supply chain-related information for the system, system component, or system service.

controls.title: Notification Agreements
controls.description: Establish agreements and procedures with entities involved in the supply chain for the system, system component, or system service for notification of supply chain compromises; and assessment of results of events or incidents that may impact supply chain risk.

controls.title: Tamper Resistance and Detection
controls.description: Implement a tamper protection program for the system, system component, or system service.

controls.title: Inspection of Systems or Components
controls.description: Inspect organization-defined systems or system components at organization-defined intervals to detect tampering.

controls.title: Component Authenticity
controls.description: Develop and implement anti-counterfeit policy and procedures that include the means to detect and prevent counterfeit components from entering the system; and report counterfeit system components to organization-defined external reporting organizations.

controls.title: Component Disposal
controls.description: Dispose of organization-defined data, documentation, tools, or system components using organization-defined techniques and methods.
